race.name: Sudety Tour
race.tagline: A challenging cross-border gran fondo through the Sudetes connecting Czechia and Poland

climate.description: May in the Broumov region typically features mild spring conditions with variable weather. The Sudetes mountains can experience unpredictable conditions with potential for rain.
challenges: Precipitation: Moderate to high; typical spring variability

terrain.surface: Challenging undulating terrain through the Sudetes mountains with constant elevation changes. The route features two major climbs and crosses the Czech-Polish border, incorporating both forest roads and cobblestone sections.
features: Challenging undulating terrain through the Sudetes mountains with constant elevation changes
The route features two major climbs and crosses the Czech-Polish border, incorporating both forest roads and cobblestone sections

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 67. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 63.

course_description.character: A challenging, undulating cross-border gran fondo through the Sudetes mountains. The route alternates between forest climbs, cobblestone sections, and rolling terrain, with constant elevation changes throughout. The grandfondo features two major climbs and incorporates both Czechia and Poland, creating a unique multinational cycling experience.
course_description.signature_challenge: The combination of the 10 km Polish forest ascent and the brutal 1 km cobblestone climb at Vysoka Srbska, separated by rolling terrain that prevents full recovery. The mediofondo shortcuts the eastern loops but retains both signature climbs.
suffering_zones: Szczebliniec Wielki forest climb (km 30-40): 10 km at 5% average gradient between Rádków and Karlów on the Polish side
Vysoka Srbska cobblestone climb (km 52): 1 km at 10% average, 15% maximum gradient on Czech terrain
Constant undulation throughout both grandfondo and mediofondo routes with no extended flat recovery sections

logistics.airport: PRG (Václav Havel Airport Prague), 180km, 2.5hr drive; PED (Pardubice Airport), 100km, 1.5hr drive
logistics.lodging_strategy: Broumov offers historic lodging options including accommodations near the Benedictine Monastery. Specific hotel partnerships and booking details unavailable in sources; contact organizers via sudetytour@czechcyclehub.cz.
logistics.transport: Sudety Tour is based in Broumov, a picturesque town in northeastern Bohemia near the Czech-Polish border. The event spans two days with a time trial in Poland (May 9) and road race in Czechia (May 10). Limited logistical infrastructure details are available; early planning is recommended.

history.origin_story: Sudety Tour was originally established in 2004 as a road cycling marathon, running annually until 2013. The event was revived in 2023 under the name Dekom System Sudety Tour with a start in Broumov and partial routing through Poland. In 2026, it becomes the first iteration of Sudety Tour to join the UCI Gran Fondo World Series.
notable_moments: 2004: Original Sudety Tour launched as road cycling marathon
2023: Revival as Dekom System Sudety Tour with cross-border routing
2026: First UCI Gran Fondo World Series iteration

biased_opinion.summary: Sudety Tour represents a compelling resurrection of a historic cycling event, now elevated to UCI Gran Fondo World Series status. The cross-border Czechia-Poland routing and two signature climbs—particularly the cobblestone Vysoka Srbska—create a genuinely challenging experience. However, the event's recent revival and limited historical track record introduce uncertainty around organization quality and field depth. For cyclists seeking a technical, elevation-rich challenge in an underexplored region with cultural attractions, this delivers. Logistics and accessibility remain the primary friction points.

final_verdict.one_liner: A technically demanding, culturally rich cross-border gran fondo with authentic climbing challenges, hampered by limited logistical infrastructure and uncertain field depth.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Sudety Tour merits consideration for experienced gran fondo cyclists seeking elevation, technical terrain, and cultural immersion. The 152 km granfondo's 2,086 m elevation gain and signature climbs—the 10 km Polish forest ascent and 1 km Czech cobblestone section—deliver genuine suffering. The cross-border routing through the Sudetes adds adventure and novelty. However, the event's 2023 revival and 2026 UCI integration mean limited historical data on execution quality, participant experience, and field competitiveness. Logistical challenges (remote location, 180 km from Prague airport, sparse accommodation details) and absent pricing information create friction. Best suited for self-sufficient cyclists with flexible schedules and interest in Eastern European cycling culture.

videos.title: UCI GranFondo 2025
videos.channel: SelfRighteousCyclist - Ride it, Write it, Show it
videos.description:
The UCI World Age Group Championships, starting from Lorne, Victoria, Australia.  The course started with a 10km climb at 4.1% and headed out into the hinterland, before returning to the range and up and over to the famous Great Ocean Road with 40km to go. All up about 1850 vertical metres.  
Racing in the Masters 6 (55-59 category) was a great experience to be riding with so many worthy competitors from around the world.  Thanks to those who travelled from far and wide to do the event. I hope the Japanese rider who fell off his bike on the climb is ok.

If you are thinking of doing Amy's Gran Fondo in the future or just looking for a challenging course, this ride will not disappoint.

videos.title: MTB Trilogy 3. Stage "Vrani Hory" 2021
videos.curation_reason: Recent stage race footage from Czech Republic with good duration for course preview content.

terrain_notes.text: Hot sticky conditions with technical sections that weave in and out of dense jungle
Course conditions that will make or break riders' chances, described as tough to adapt to

gear_mentions.text: Flat tires and gear problems are common challenges riders will have to face during the race

race_day_tips.text: Riders need to prepare for dehydration and heat stroke as major challenges during the race

additional_quotes.text: Sometimes being the underdog of the race isn't so bad while the crowd focuses on the favorites the underdog rides on like a fighter with nothing to lose

rider_intel.search_text: The Dekom System Sudety Tour transcripts reveal limited specific information about this Czech gravel race. One video discusses a different mountain bike event in Lanka featuring hot, sticky conditions with technical jungle sections. That race involved dehydration, heat stroke, gear problems, and flat tires as major challenges for riders navigating technical terrain through dense jungle areas. The course was described as tough to adapt to, with conditions that could make or break qualification chances. However, the available transcripts do not contain specific details about the Broumov, Czechia course conditions, terrain characteristics, or race-specific challenges that riders would face during the 93-mile Sudety Tour event.
